note_data.title: Translation (ترجمہ)
keyPoints: **Translation** (ترجمہ) means conveying the exact meaning from one language to another while maintaining the original sense and context
**Literal translation** (لفظی ترجمہ) focuses on word-for-word conversion but may lose the natural flow of the target language
**Free translation** (آزاد ترجمہ) prioritizes conveying the meaning and spirit of the original text over literal word conversion
**Accuracy** (درستگی) in translation requires understanding both the source language grammar and target language syntax
**Context** (سیاق و سباق) must be preserved - the same word may have different meanings in different situations
**Cultural adaptation** (ثقافتی موافقت) involves adjusting expressions and idioms that don't directly translate between languages
**Grammatical structures** (گرامری ڈھانچے) must be adapted to fit the target language's natural sentence construction
**Tense consistency** (زمانے کی یکسانی) must be maintained throughout the translation to preserve the timeline of events

definitions.definition: The process of converting text from one language to another while preserving the original meaning, context, and intent
Word-for-word conversion from source to target language, maintaining the exact structure of the original
Translation that focuses on conveying the overall meaning and natural flow rather than exact word-for-word conversion
The circumstances and background information that give meaning to words and phrases in translation

howToAnswer.instruction: Convert the given text maintaining accuracy of meaning, proper grammar, and natural flow in the target language
Give the translation directly without explanation or additional commentary
Provide the translation and explain why certain choices were made, using 'because' to justify translation decisions

modelAnswer.question: Translate the following English sentence into Urdu: 'The students worked hard throughout the year to achieve their goals.'
modelAnswer.answer: طلباء نے سارا سال محنت کی تاکہ وہ اپنے مقاصد حاصل کر سکیں۔
markBreakdown: Mark 1: Correct translation of 'students worked hard' (طلباء نے محنت کی)
Mark 2: Accurate time expression 'throughout the year' (سارا سال)
Mark 3: Proper purpose clause 'to achieve' (تاکہ حاصل کر سکیں)
Mark 4: Correct possessive and object 'their goals' (اپنے مقاصد)

commonMistakes: Never translate idioms word-for-word — always find the equivalent expression in the target language
Students often ignore gender and number agreement when translating from English to Urdu
Avoid mixing formal and informal registers within the same translation
Never leave English sentence structure when translating into Urdu — adapt to Urdu grammar patterns
Students frequently forget to use appropriate honorifics and respect levels in Urdu translations
examinerTips: Translation questions appear in both Paper 1 and Paper 2 components
Always read the entire passage before starting translation to understand the context
Check your translation by reading it aloud to ensure it sounds natural in the target language
Pay special attention to tense consistency throughout longer translation passages
note_data.keyDiagramDescription: No specific diagram required for this topic, but understanding sentence structure flow charts can help organize complex translations
